```{R save-Indicies-Plot-None-Transformation}
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$outwas_none, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig6.1d_outcomes_diabetes", 
       prefix_subsets = c("DIA", "CVD"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 5.25, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

  list(df = datasets$outwas_none, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "figS6_otu_outcomes_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 85.5, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8)
)

##################################################
# GENERATE ALL INDICES PLOTS WITH UPDATED STRUCTURE
##################################################

# Generate all indices plots using the updated unified function
output_dir <- "none"  # Add this line to match Terry plots
indices_success_count <- 0
for (i in seq_along(indices_plot_configs)) {
  config <- indices_plot_configs[[i]]
  
  cat("Indices Plot", i, "of", length(indices_plot_configs), ":\n")
  
  # Call the unified plotting function with all Terry plot parameters
  result <- plot_indices(
    df = config$df,                                              # Dataset to plot
    plot_type = config$plot_type,                                # "otu" or "host"
    file_prefix = file.path(output_dir, config$file_prefix),     # Output filename prefix with directory
    width_mm = config$width_mm,                                  # Plot width in mm
    calibrate_y_mm = config$calibrate_y_mm %||% 0,               # Manual height calibration
    mapping_data = ubiome_genus_mapping_complete,                # Indices mapping data
    genus_mapping = genus_mapping,                               # OTU to genus mapping
    ubiome_variable_mapping = ubiome_variable_mapping,           # Host variable descriptions
    prefix_subsets = config$prefix_subsets,                      # Filter to specific prefixes
    remove_na = config$remove_na %||% FALSE,                     # Remove NA entries
    remove_na_unclassified = config$remove_na_unclassified %||% FALSE,  # Remove unclassified microbes
    show_legend = config$show_legend %||% TRUE,                  # Legend display
    dummy_top_bottom = config$dummy_top_bottom %||% "split",     # Dummy positioning
    hide_axis_title = config$hide_axis_title %||% FALSE,         # Hide axis titles
    geom_point_shape = config$geom_point_shape %||% 21,          # Point shape
    geom_point_size = config$geom_point_size %||% 3.5           # Point size
  )
  
  # Track successful plots
  if (!is.null(result)) {
    indices_success_count <- indices_success_count + 1
    # cat("  ✓ SUCCESS\n\n")
  } else {
    # cat("  ✗ FAILED (no data or error)\n\n")
  }
}

# cat("=== INDICES PLOT GENERATION COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Successfully generated:", indices_success_count, "out of", length(indices_plot_configs), "plots\n")
# cat("Output directory: ./", output_dir, "/\n")
cat("Generated", indices_success_count, "of", length(indices_plot_configs), "indices plots successfully.\n")
```

```{R save-Indicies-Plot-Hellinger-Transformation}
library(dplyr)
##################################################
# INDICES PLOT CONFIGURATIONS
##################################################
indices_plot_configs <- list(
  # OTU plots - show microbial genera (Y-axis) vs host variable types (colors), no annotations for clarity
  # HOST plots - show host variables (Y-axis) vs microbial phylums (colors), with genus annotations
  # FIGURE 1 ----
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "figS1_host_demographics_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 17.6, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig1.1b_demographics_poverty_edu", 
       prefix_subsets = c("IND","EDU"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 8.3, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig1.1a_demographics_poverty_edu", 
       prefix_subsets = c("RIA","ETH","BOR"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 9.5, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig1.1c_demographics_age", 
       prefix_subsets = c("RID","AGE"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 5.35, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "figS1_otu_demographics_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 95, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

  # FIGURE 2 ----
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig2_host_demographics_age", 
       prefix_subsets = c("RID"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 4.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig2_host_demographics_age_squared", 
       prefix_subsets = c("AGE_SQUARED"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 4.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$demowas_hellinger, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "figS2_otu_demographics_age", 
       prefix_subsets = c("RID","AGE"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 53, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, color_by_variable = TRUE, 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

  # FIGURE 3 ----
  list(df = datasets$oradwas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig3_host_oral_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 7.4, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),
  
  list(df = datasets$oradwas_hellinger, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "figS3_otu_oral_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 71, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

  # FIGURE 4 ----
  list(df = datasets$exwas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "figS4_host_exposome_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 72, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = FALSE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

  list(df = datasets$exwas_hellinger, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig4.2b_exposome_diet_macro_energy", 
       prefix_subsets = macro_energy_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 8.3, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8),

##################################################
# GENERATE ALL INDICES PLOTS WITH UPDATED STRUCTURE
##################################################

# Generate all indices plots using the updated unified function
output_dir <- "hellinger"  # Add this line to match Terry plots
indices_success_count <- 0
for (i in seq_along(indices_plot_configs)) {
  config <- indices_plot_configs[[i]]
  
  cat("Indices Plot", i, "of", length(indices_plot_configs), ":\n")
  
  # Call the unified plotting function with all Terry plot parameters
  result <- plot_indices(
    df = config$df,                                              # Dataset to plot
    plot_type = config$plot_type,                                # "otu" or "host"
    file_prefix = file.path(output_dir, config$file_prefix),     # Output filename prefix with directory
    width_mm = config$width_mm,                                  # Plot width in mm
    calibrate_y_mm = config$calibrate_y_mm %||% 0,               # Manual height calibration
    mapping_data = ubiome_genus_mapping_complete,                # Indices mapping data
    genus_mapping = genus_mapping,                               # OTU to genus mapping
    ubiome_variable_mapping = ubiome_variable_mapping,           # Host variable descriptions
    prefix_subsets = config$prefix_subsets,                      # Filter to specific prefixes
    remove_na = config$remove_na %||% FALSE,                     # Remove NA entries
    remove_na_unclassified = config$remove_na_unclassified %||% FALSE,  # Remove unclassified microbes
    show_legend = config$show_legend %||% TRUE,                  # Legend display
    dummy_top_bottom = config$dummy_top_bottom %||% "split",     # Dummy positioning
    hide_axis_title = config$hide_axis_title %||% FALSE,         # Hide axis titles
    geom_point_shape = config$geom_point_shape %||% 21,          # Point shape
    geom_point_size = config$geom_point_size %||% 3.5           # Point size
  )
  
  # Track successful plots
  if (!is.null(result)) {
    indices_success_count <- indices_success_count + 1
    # cat("  ✓ SUCCESS\n\n")
  } else {
    # cat("  ✗ FAILED (no data or error)\n\n")
  }
}
# cat("=== INDICES PLOT GENERATION COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Successfully generated:", indices_success_count, "out of", length(indices_plot_configs), "plots\n")
# cat("Output directory: ./", output_dir, "/\n")
cat("Generated", indices_success_count, "of", length(indices_plot_configs), "indices plots successfully.\n")
```

```{R save-Indicies-Plot-CLR-Transformation}
library(dplyr)
##################################################
# INDICES PLOT CONFIGURATIONS
##################################################
indices_plot_configs <- list(
  # OTU plots - show microbial genera (Y-axis) vs host variable types (colors), no annotations for clarity
  # HOST plots - show host variables (Y-axis) vs microbial phylums (colors), with genus annotations
  # FIGURE 1 ----
  list(df = datasets$demowas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "figS1_host_demographics_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 17.6, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$demowas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig1.1b_demographics_poverty_edu", 
       prefix_subsets = c("IND","EDU"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 8.3, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

       prefix_subsets = blood_onecarbon_folate_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 8.3, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.4d_phenome_Immune_blood_oncology_prostate_vars", 
       prefix_subsets = blood_oncology_prostate_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 6.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.4e_phenome_Immune_blood_immune_inflammation_vars",
       prefix_subsets = blood_immune_inflammation_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 5.35, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.3a_phenome_physiology_body_composition_dxa", 
       prefix_subsets = c("DXA", "DXX"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 13, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.1a_phenome_hematology_blood_cell_count_vars", 
       prefix_subsets = blood_cell_count_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 8.3, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.4c_phenome_Immune_serum_clinical_vars", 
       prefix_subsets = serum_clinical_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 6.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.2d_phenome_endocrine_folate_status_vars", 
       prefix_subsets = folate_status_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 5.35, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.4b_phenome_Immune_urine_kidney_iodine_vars", 
       prefix_subsets = urine_kidney_iodine_vars, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 6.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.4a_phenome_Immune_airway_inflammation_var", 
       prefix_subsets = airway_inflammation_var, width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 6.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig5.3b_phenome_physiology_anthropometry_blood_pressure", 
       prefix_subsets = c("BMX", "BPX", "MDI", "MSY"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 15.1, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

  list(df = datasets$phewas_clr, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "figS5_otu_phenome_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 125, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

  # FIGURE 6 ----
  list(df = datasets$outwas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "figS6_host_outcomes_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 15.5, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$outwas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig6.1c_outcomes_cancer", 
       prefix_subsets = c("CAN"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 5.35, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$outwas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig6.1a_outcomes_cardiovascular", 
       prefix_subsets = c("CHD", "HEA", "ANG", "STR", "CVD"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 9.5, 
       remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$outwas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig6.1b_outcomes_respiratory", 
       prefix_subsets = c("EMP", "AST", "BRO"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 6.2, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),
  
  list(df = datasets$outwas_clr, plot_type = "host", file_prefix = "fig6.1d_outcomes_diabetes", 
       prefix_subsets = c("DIA", "CVD"), width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 5.35, remove_na = TRUE, 
       remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, show_legend = TRUE, dummy_top_bottom = "top", 
       hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7),

  list(df = datasets$outwas_clr, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "figS6_otu_outcomes_full",
       width_mm = 140, calibrate_y_mm = 85.5, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
       show_legend = TRUE, hide_axis_title = TRUE, geom_point_shape = 21, geom_point_size = 1.8+1.7)
)

##################################################
# GENERATE ALL INDICES PLOTS WITH UPDATED STRUCTURE
##################################################

# Generate all indices plots using the updated unified function
output_dir <- "clr"  # Add this line to match Terry plots
indices_success_count <- 0
for (i in seq_along(indices_plot_configs)) {
  config <- indices_plot_configs[[i]]
  
  cat("Indices Plot", i, "of", length(indices_plot_configs), ":\n")
  
  # Call the unified plotting function with all Terry plot parameters
  result <- plot_indices(
    df = config$df,                                              # Dataset to plot
    plot_type = config$plot_type,                                # "otu" or "host"
    file_prefix = file.path(output_dir, config$file_prefix),     # Output filename prefix with directory
    width_mm = config$width_mm,                                  # Plot width in mm
    calibrate_y_mm = config$calibrate_y_mm %||% 0,               # Manual height calibration
    mapping_data = ubiome_genus_mapping_complete,                # Indices mapping data
    genus_mapping = genus_mapping,                               # OTU to genus mapping
    ubiome_variable_mapping = ubiome_variable_mapping,           # Host variable descriptions
    prefix_subsets = config$prefix_subsets,                      # Filter to specific prefixes
    remove_na = config$remove_na %||% FALSE,                     # Remove NA entries
    remove_na_unclassified = config$remove_na_unclassified %||% FALSE,  # Remove unclassified microbes
    show_legend = config$show_legend %||% TRUE,                  # Legend display
    dummy_top_bottom = config$dummy_top_bottom %||% "split",     # Dummy positioning
    hide_axis_title = config$hide_axis_title %||% FALSE,         # Hide axis titles
    geom_point_shape = config$geom_point_shape %||% 21,          # Point shape
    geom_point_size = config$geom_point_size %||% 3.5           # Point size
  )
  
  # Track successful plots
  if (!is.null(result)) {
    indices_success_count <- indices_success_count + 1
    # cat("  ✓ SUCCESS\n\n")
  } else {
    # cat("  ✗ FAILED (no data or error)\n\n")
  }
}
# cat("=== INDICES PLOT GENERATION COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Successfully generated:", indices_success_count, "out of", length(indices_plot_configs), "plots\n")
# cat("Output directory: ./", output_dir, "/\n")
cat("Generated", indices_success_count, "of", length(indices_plot_configs), "indices plots successfully.\n")
```

































```{R Change of plans. Make a separate sample_data with categorical variables with Factor level, and reference}
# Variable configuration files
var_files <- c(
  "demoWAS" = file.path(config_dir_path, "1_demoWAS_vars.txt"),
  "oradWAS" = file.path(config_dir_path, "2_oradWAS_vars.txt"),
  "exWAS" = file.path(config_dir_path, "3_exWAS_vars.txt"),
  "pheWAS" = file.path(config_dir_path, "4_pheWAS_vars.txt"),
  "outWAS" = file.path(config_dir_path, "5_outWAS_vars.txt"),
  "zimWAS" = file.path(config_dir_path, "6_zimWAS_vars.txt")
)

# Load all variables from config files
load_vars <- function(file_path) {
  if (!file.exists(file_path)) return(character(0))
  vars <- readLines(file_path, warn = FALSE)
  vars[nzchar(vars) & !grepl("^#", vars)]  # Remove empty lines and comments
}


nhanes_id_col <- c("SEQN")
nhanes_weight_cols = c() # c("SDDSRVYR","WTINT2YR","WTMEC2YR","SDMVPSU","SDMVSTRA") # commented for now, since not needed for heatmap

# in case we need them later
nhanes_weight_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% 
  dplyr::select(SEQN, SDDSRVYR,WTINT2YR, WTMEC2YR, SDMVPSU, SDMVSTRA)

demoWAS_vars <- purrr::map(var_files, load_vars)$demoWAS %>% unique()
demoWAS_vars <- c(nhanes_id_col, nhanes_weight_cols, demoWAS_vars)

oradWAS_vars <- purrr::map(var_files, load_vars)$oradWAS %>% unique()
oradWAS_vars <- c(nhanes_id_col, nhanes_weight_cols, oradWAS_vars)

exWAS_vars <- purrr::map(var_files, load_vars)$exWAS %>% unique()
exWAS_vars <- c(nhanes_id_col, nhanes_weight_cols, exWAS_vars)

pheWAS_vars <- purrr::map(var_files, load_vars)$pheWAS %>% unique()
pheWAS_vars <- c(nhanes_id_col, nhanes_weight_cols, pheWAS_vars)

outWAS_vars <- purrr::map(var_files, load_vars)$outWAS %>% unique()
outWAS_vars <- c(nhanes_id_col, nhanes_weight_cols, outWAS_vars)

zimWAS_vars <- purrr::map(var_files, load_vars)$zimWAS %>% unique()
zimWAS_vars <- c(nhanes_id_col, nhanes_weight_cols, zimWAS_vars)


demoWAS_vars %>% str()
oradWAS_vars %>% str()                
exWAS_vars %>% str()
pheWAS_vars %>% str()
outWAS_vars %>% str()
zimWAS_vars %>% str()

sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% dplyr::select(demoWAS_vars)
sample_data_oradWAS_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% dplyr::select(oradWAS_vars)
sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% dplyr::select(exWAS_vars)
sample_data_pheWAS_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% dplyr::select(pheWAS_vars)
sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% dplyr::select(outWAS_vars)
sample_data_zimWAS_vars_subset <- base::data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "SEQN") %>% dplyr::select(zimWAS_vars)

sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset %>% dim()
```

```{R}
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(tibble)

# 1. Extract predefined factor variables
pre_defined_factors <- data.frame(sample_data(ubiome_relative_none)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("SEQN") %>%        # make the rownames the SEQN column
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor)) %>%  # turn any character cols into factors
  dplyr::select(SEQN,
         Gender,
         AgeGroup,
         EducationLevel,
         Ethnicity,
         US_Born,
         Household_Size_Factor,
         Marital_Status,
         Interview_Language)

# 2. Join and factorize for heatmap
demoWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap <- sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset %>%
  left_join(pre_defined_factors, by = "SEQN") %>%
  mutate(
    # Relevel gender: Female as reference
    Gender = fct_relevel(Gender, "Female"),
    
    # > median(sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset$RIDAGEYR, na.rm = TRUE)
    # [1] 39
    Age_group = fct_relevel(factor(AgeGroup), "30-39"),
    
    # > pre_defined_factors %>% count(EducationLevel, sort = TRUE)
    #     EducationLevel    n
    # 1       College/AA 2452
    # 2     9-11th Grade 2153
    # 3 College Graduate 1955
    # 4      High School 1934
    # 5      < 9th Grade 1162
    Education_level = factor(EducationLevel,
                             levels = c("< 9th Grade",
                                        "9-11th Grade",
                                        "High School",
                                        "College/AA",
                                        "College Graduate")) %>%
      fct_relevel("College/AA"),
    
    Ethnicity = fct_relevel(Ethnicity, "White"),
    
    US_born = fct_relevel(US_Born, "US Born"),
    
    # > median(sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset$INDFMPIR, na.rm = TRUE)
    # [1] 1.81
    Income_to_poverty_ratio = case_when(
      INDFMPIR < 0.50                       ~ "Below 50%",
      INDFMPIR >= 0.50 & INDFMPIR < 1.00    ~ "50–99%",
      INDFMPIR >= 1.00 & INDFMPIR < 1.25    ~ "100–124%",
      INDFMPIR >= 1.25 & INDFMPIR < 1.50    ~ "125–149%",
      INDFMPIR >= 1.50 & INDFMPIR < 1.85    ~ "150–184%",
      INDFMPIR >= 1.85 & INDFMPIR < 2.00    ~ "185–199%",
      INDFMPIR >= 2.00                      ~ "200% and Over",
      TRUE                                  ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c("Below 50%",
                        "50–99%",
                        "100–124%",
                        "125–149%",
                        "150–184%",
                        "185–199%",
                        "200% and Over")) %>%
      fct_relevel("150–184%"),
    
    Household_size = factor(Household_Size_Factor,
                            levels = c("1","2","3","4","5","6","7+")) %>%
      fct_relevel("4"),
    
    # > pre_defined_factors %>% count(Marital_Status, sort = TRUE)
    #        Marital_Status    n
    # 1             Married 4026
    Marital_status = fct_relevel(Marital_Status, "Married"),
    
    # > pre_defined_factors %>% count(Interview_Language, sort = TRUE)
    #   Interview_Language    n
    # 1            English 8517
    Interview_language = fct_relevel(Interview_Language, "English")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(SEQN,
         Gender,
         Age_group,
         Education_level,
         Ethnicity,
         US_born,
         Income_to_poverty_ratio,
         Household_size,
         Marital_status,
         Interview_language)

demoWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap
demoWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap %>% str()
```


```{R}
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(tibble)
# > sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset %>% pivot_longer(-SEQN, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
# +     count(variable, value) %>%  pivot_wider(names_from = value, values_from  = n,  values_fill  = 0 )
# # A tibble: 16 × 4
#    variable            `0`   `1`  `NA`
#    <chr>             <int> <int> <int>
#  1 ANGINA             4037    70  5740
#  2 ASTHMA             4191   764  4892
#  3 BRONCHITIS         3900   205  5742
#  4 CANCER_BREAST      3864    33  5950
#  5 CANCER_COLON       3864    10  5973
#  6 CANCER_ESOPHAGEAL  3864     0  5983
#  7 CANCER_LUNG        3864     3  5980
#  8 CANCER_MOUTH       3864     0  5983
#  9 CANCER_PROSTATE    3864    24  5959
# 10 CHD                4048    60  5739
# 11 CVD                3822   270  5755
# 12 DIABETES           4474   484  4889
# 13 EMPHYSEMA          4044    69  5734
# 14 HEART_ATTACK       3987   117  5743
# 15 HEART_FAILURE      4018    94  5735
# 16 STROKE             4008    97  5742

# > sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset %>% rowwise() %>% mutate(n_reported = sum(c_across(-SEQN) == 1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% ungroup() %>% 
# +     count(n_reported) %>% arrange(n_reported)
# # A tibble: 10 × 2
#    n_reported     n
#         <int> <int>
#  1          0  8446
#  2          1   950
#  3          2   225
#  4          3   105
#  5          4    59
#  6          5    41
#  7          6     9
#  8          7     8
#  9          8     3
# 10         10     1

outWAS_vars <- setdiff(colnames(sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset),"SEQN")
oradWAS_vars <- setdiff(colnames(sample_data_oradWAS_vars_subset),"SEQN")

SEQN_outWAS_control <- sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset %>% 
    # filter for rows where EVERY variable is literally zero (no NAs allowed)
    filter(if_all(all_of(outWAS_vars),
                  ~ !is.na(.x) & .x == 0)) %>%
    pull(SEQN) # pull just the SEQN column as a vector

SEQN_oradWAS_control <- sample_data_oradWAS_vars_subset %>% 
    # filter for rows where EVERY variable is literally zero (no NAs allowed)
    filter(if_all(all_of(oradWAS_vars),
                  ~ !is.na(.x) & .x == 0)) %>%
    pull(SEQN) # pull just the SEQN column as a vector

# OUT-WAS → manual factorization
outdWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap <- sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset %>%
  mutate(
    Asthma = case_when(
      ASTHMA == 1 ~ "Asthma",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Asthma")),

    Bronchitis = case_when(
      BRONCHITIS == 1 ~ "Bronchitis",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE            ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Bronchitis")),

    Emphysema = case_when(
      EMPHYSEMA == 1 ~ "Emphysema",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE           ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Emphysema")),

    Angina = case_when(
      ANGINA == 1 ~ "Angina",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Angina")),

    Heart_failure = case_when(
      HEART_FAILURE == 1 ~ "Heart failure",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE               ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Heart failure")),

    Heart_attack = case_when(
      HEART_ATTACK == 1 ~ "Heart attack",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE              ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Heart attack")),

    Stroke = case_when(
      STROKE == 1 ~ "Stroke",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Stroke")),

    CHD = case_when(
      CHD == 1 ~ "CHD",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE     ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "CHD")),

    CVD = case_when(
      CVD == 1 ~ "CVD",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE      ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "CVD")),

    Breast_cancer = case_when(
      CANCER_BREAST == 1 ~ "Breast cancer",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE               ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Breast cancer")),

    Colon_cancer = case_when(
      CANCER_COLON == 1 ~ "Colon cancer",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE              ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Colon cancer")),

    Lung_cancer = case_when(
      CANCER_LUNG == 1 ~ "Lung cancer",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE             ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Lung cancer")),

    Esophageal_cancer = case_when(
      CANCER_ESOPHAGEAL == 1 ~ "Esophageal cancer",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE                   ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Esophageal cancer")),

    Prostate_cancer = case_when(
      CANCER_PROSTATE == 1 ~ "Prostate cancer",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE                 ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Prostate cancer")),

    Diabetes = case_when(
      DIABETES == 1 ~ "Diabetes",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_outWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE          ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Diabetes"))
  ) %>% dplyr::select(SEQN, Asthma, Bronchitis, Emphysema, Angina,Heart_failure, Heart_attack, Stroke, 
             Breast_cancer, Colon_cancer, Lung_cancer, Esophageal_cancer, Prostate_cancer, Diabetes)

# ORAD-WAS → manual factorization
oradWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap <- sample_data_oradWAS_vars_subset %>%
  mutate(
    Denture = case_when(
      DENTURE_OHAROCDE == 1 ~ "Denture",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_oradWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE                  ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Denture")),

    Gum_disease = case_when(
      GUM_DISEASE_OHAROCGP == 1 ~ "Gum disease",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_oradWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE                      ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Gum disease")),

    Oral_hygene = case_when(
      ORAL_HYGIENE_OHAROCOH == 1 ~ "Poor oral hygiene",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_oradWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE                       ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Poor oral hygiene")),

    Tooth_decay = case_when(
      TOOTH_DECAY_OHAROCDT == 1 ~ "Tooth decay",
      SEQN %in% SEQN_oradWAS_control ~ "control",
      TRUE                      ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("control", "Tooth decay"))
  ) %>% dplyr::select(SEQN, Denture, Gum_disease, Oral_hygene, Tooth_decay)


outdWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap
oradWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap

# outdWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap %>% pivot_longer(-SEQN, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>% count(variable, value) %>%  pivot_wider(names_from = value, values_from  = n,  values_fill  = 0 )
# oradWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap %>% pivot_longer(-SEQN, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>% count(variable, value) %>%  pivot_wider(names_from = value, values_from  = n,  values_fill  = 0 )
```


```{R Phewas and Exwas}
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(tibble)

exWAS_vars %>% str()
pheWAS_vars %>% str()
sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset %>% data.table::as.data.table()
sample_data_pheWAS_vars_subset %>% data.table::as.data.table()
sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset %>% dim()
sample_data_pheWAS_vars_subset %>% dim()

exWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap <- 
  sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset %>%
  left_join(
    sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset %>% 
      dplyr::select(SEQN, RIDAGEYR, AGE_SQUARED, RIAGENDR),
    by = "SEQN"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Hepatitis B surface antigen: 1=Positive, 2=Negative
    Hepatitis_B_surface_antigen = case_when(
      LBDHBG == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDHBG == 2 ~ "Negative",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # Hepatitis C antibody: 1=Positive, 2=Negative
    Hepatitis_C_antibody = case_when(
      LBDHCV == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDHCV == 2 ~ "Negative",
      # LBDHCV == 5 ~ "Indeterminate", # TREAT AS NA
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # Hepatitis D (anti-HDV): 1=Positive, 2=Negative
    Hepatitis_D_anti_HDV = case_when(
      LBDHD == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDHD == 2 ~ "Negative",
      TRUE       ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # Hepatitis E IgG: 1=Positive, 2=Negative, 3=Indeterminate
    Hepatitis_E_IgG = case_when(
      LBDHEG == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDHEG == 2 ~ "Negative",
      # LBDHEG == 3 ~ "Indeterminate", # TREAT AS NA
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # Hepatitis E IgM: 1=Positive, 2=Negative
    Hepatitis_E_IgM = case_when(
      LBDHEM == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDHEM == 2 ~ "Negative",
      # LBDHEM == 3 ~ "Indeterminate", # TREAT AS NA
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # HPV PCR summary: 1=Positive, 2=Negative, 3=Inadequate
    HPV_PCR_summary = case_when(
      LBDRPCR == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDRPCR == 2 ~ "Negative",
      # LBDRPCR == 3 ~ "Inadequate", # TREAT AS NA
      TRUE         ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # Varicella antibody: 1=Positive, 2=Negative
    Varicella_antibody = case_when(
      LBDVWCGP == 1 ~ "Positive",
      LBDVWCGP == 2 ~ "Negative",
      TRUE          ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    
    # smoking <- sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset %>% select(SEQN,SMQ710,SMQ720,SMQ725,SMQ_current_ever_never,SMQ_how_long_since_quit_years) 
    # 
    # smoking %>% pivot_longer(-SEQN, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
    # count(variable, value) %>%  pivot_wider(names_from = value, values_from  = n,  values_fill  = 0 )

    # 1) Days smoked in last 5 days (0–5), with "Never smoked" as ref
    Days_smoked_in_5_days = case_when(
      SMQ_current_ever_never == 0           ~ "Never smoked",
      SMQ710 >= 1   & SMQ710 <= 5           ~ as.character(SMQ710),
      TRUE                                  ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c("Never smoked", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5")) %>%
      fct_relevel("Never smoked"),
    
    # 2) Cigarettes per day (1–95), keep numeric levels
    # > with(filter(sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset, SMQ720!=999), quantile(SMQ720, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE))
    #   0%  20%  40%  60%  80% 100% 
    #    1    3    6   10   20   95 
    Cigarettes_per_day = case_when(
      SMQ_current_ever_never == 0 ~ "Never smoked",
      SMQ720 >= 1  & SMQ720 <= 3   ~ "1–3 per day",
      SMQ720 > 3  & SMQ720 <= 6    ~ "4–6 per day",
      SMQ720 > 6  & SMQ720 <= 10   ~ "7–10 per day",
      SMQ720 > 10 & SMQ720 <= 20   ~ "11–20 per day",
      SMQ720 > 20 & SMQ720 <= 95   ~ "20+ per day",
      SMQ720 == 999 | is.na(SMQ720) ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c(
        "Never smoked",
        "1–3 per day",
        "4–6 per day",
        "7–10 per day",
        "11–20 per day",
        "20+ per day"
      )),
    
    # 3) When last smoked: Today / Yesterday / 3–5 days ago
    Last_smoked_cigarette = case_when(
      SMQ725 == 1 ~ "Today",
      SMQ725 == 2 ~ "Yesterday",
      SMQ725 == 3 ~ "3–5 days ago",
      TRUE           ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c("Today", "Yesterday", "3–5 days ago")),
    
    # 4) Ever/never/current smoking status, with "Never smoker" as ref
    Smoking_status = case_when(
      SMQ_current_ever_never == 0 ~ "Never smoker",
      SMQ_current_ever_never == 2 ~ "Former smoker",
      SMQ_current_ever_never == 1 ~ "Current smoker",
      TRUE                        ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c("Never smoker", "Former smoker", "Current smoker")) %>%
      fct_relevel("Never smoker"),
    
    )

# exWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap %>% select(SEQN, Days_smoked_in_5_days, Cigarettes_per_day, Smoking_status) %>% pivot_longer(-SEQN, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>% count(variable, value) %>%  pivot_wider(names_from = value, values_from  = n,  values_fill  = 0 )

pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap <- sample_data_pheWAS_vars_subset %>%
  left_join(
    sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset %>%
      dplyr::select(SEQN, RIDAGEYR, AGE_SQUARED, RIAGENDR), # for computing gender/age aware categories
    by = "SEQN") %>%
  mutate(
 
    BMI_category = case_when(
      # https://www.cdc.gov/bmi/adult-calculator/bmi-categories.html
      BMXBMI < 18.5                    ~ "Underweight",
      BMXBMI >= 18.5 & BMXBMI < 25     ~ "Healthy weight", # reference
      BMXBMI >= 25   & BMXBMI < 30     ~ "Overweight",
      BMXBMI >= 30   & BMXBMI < 35     ~ "Class 1 Obesity",
      # BMXBMI >= 35   & BMXBMI < 40     ~ "Class 2 Obesity",
      # BMXBMI >= 40                    ~ "Class 3 Obesity",
      BMXBMI >= 35                    ~ "Class 2-3 Obesity",
      TRUE                             ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c("Underweight","Healthy weight","Overweight","Class 1 Obesity","Class 2-3 Obesity")) %>% 
      fct_relevel("Healthy weight%"), # reference
    
    # > quintiles <- with(sample_data_pheWAS_vars_subset, quantile(BPXPLS, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE))
    # > quintiles
    #   0%  20%  40%  60%  80% 100% 
    #   40   62   70   76   82  126 
    pulse_category = case_when(
      BPXPLS < 60                     ~ "<60 bpm",
      BPXPLS >= 60  & BPXPLS < 70     ~ "60–70 bpm",
      BPXPLS >= 70  & BPXPLS < 75     ~ "70–75 bpm",
      BPXPLS >= 75  & BPXPLS < 85     ~ "75–85 bpm",
      BPXPLS >= 85                    ~ "85+ bpm",
      TRUE                            ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c(
        "<60 bpm",
        "60–70 bpm",
        "70–75 bpm", # reference
        "75–85 bpm",
        "85+ bpm"
      )) %>%
      fct_relevel("70–75 bpm"), # reference
    
    Blood_pressure = case_when(
      # 1) Hypertensive Crisis
      MSYSTOLIC  > 180 | MDIASTOLIC > 120 ~ "Hypertensive Crisis",
      # 2) Stage 2 Hypertension
      MSYSTOLIC >= 140 | MDIASTOLIC >=  90 ~ "Hypertension Stage 2",
      # 3) Stage 1 Hypertension
      (MSYSTOLIC >= 130 & MSYSTOLIC < 140) |
      (MDIASTOLIC >=  80 & MDIASTOLIC <  90) ~ "Hypertension Stage 1",
      # 4) Elevated
      (MSYSTOLIC >= 120 & MSYSTOLIC < 130) &
      (MDIASTOLIC <  80)                  ~ "Elevated",
      # 5) Normal
      MSYSTOLIC < 120 & MDIASTOLIC <  80  ~ "Normal",
      # Fallback
      TRUE                                 ~ NA_character_
    ) %>%
    factor(levels = c( 
      "Normal",  # reference
      "Elevated",
      "Hypertension Stage 1",
      "Hypertension Stage 2",
      "Hypertensive Crisis"
    )) %>%
      fct_relevel("Normal"), # reference
  
  # > with(filter(pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap, RIAGENDR==1), quantile(BMXWAIST, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE))
  #     0%    20%    40%    60%    80%   100% 
  #  58.10  82.06  92.70 100.40 110.90 179.00 
  # > with(filter(pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap, RIAGENDR==2), quantile(BMXWAIST, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE))
  #    0%   20%   40%   60%   80%  100% 
  #  58.6  80.2  89.2  97.9 109.3 165.0 
  # Hard‐coded whole‐number quintile breakpoints:
  #  Male:   0%:58.10, 20%:82.06, 40%:92.70, 60%:100.40, 80%:110.90, 100%:179.00 
  #  Fem:    0%:58.6,  20%:80.2,  40%:89.2,  60%:97.9,   80%:109.3,  100%:165.0
  #  We’ll take floor() of each value for simplicity.
  
      Waist_circumference_quintiles = case_when(
        # Q1 (0–20%)
        (RIAGENDR == 1 & BMXWAIST >= 58  & BMXWAIST <= 82) |
        (RIAGENDR == 2 & BMXWAIST >= 58  & BMXWAIST <= 80) 
          ~ "Male 58-82cm, Female 58-80cm",
        
        # Q2 (20–40%)
        (RIAGENDR == 1 & BMXWAIST >  82  & BMXWAIST <= 92) |
        (RIAGENDR == 2 & BMXWAIST >  80  & BMXWAIST <= 89) 
          ~ "Male 83-92cm, Female 81-89cm",
        
        # Q3 (40–60%)
        (RIAGENDR == 1 & BMXWAIST >  92  & BMXWAIST <= 100) |
        (RIAGENDR == 2 & BMXWAIST >  89  & BMXWAIST <= 97) 
          ~ "Male 93-100cm, Female 90-97cm",
        
        # Q4 (60–80%)
        (RIAGENDR == 1 & BMXWAIST >  100 & BMXWAIST <= 110) |
        (RIAGENDR == 2 & BMXWAIST >  97  & BMXWAIST <= 109) 
          ~ "Male 101-110cm, Female 98-109cm",
        
        # Q5 (80–100%)
        (RIAGENDR == 1 & BMXWAIST >  110 & BMXWAIST <= 179) |
        (RIAGENDR == 2 & BMXWAIST >  109 & BMXWAIST <= 165) 
          ~ "Male 111-179cm, Female 110-165cm",
        
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ) %>%
      factor(levels = c(
        "Male 58-82cm, Female 58-80cm",
        "Male 83-92cm, Female 81-89cm",
        "Male 93-100cm, Female 90-97cm",
        "Male 101-110cm, Female 98-109cm",
        "Male 111-179cm, Female 110-165cm"
      ))
    )

pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap %>% count(BMI_category, sort = TRUE)

with(filter(pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap, RIAGENDR==1), quantile(BMXWAIST, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE))
with(filter(pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap, RIAGENDR==2), quantile(BMXWAIST, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE))


variable_description %>% dplyr::select(Variable.Name, Variable.Description) %>% filter(Variable.Name %in% colnames(pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap) & 
         grepl("LBX", Variable.Name)) %>% filter(!duplicated(Variable.Name))

ubiome_variable_mapping %>% filter(grepl("LBX", var_name)) %>% filter(!duplicated(var_description))

```

Looking at your heatmap code structure, I'll create a comprehensive network plotting system that generates **one network plot per host variable** (just like the heatmaps), using the OrBl_div color scale for edge coloring based on association percentages.

```{r}
################################################################################
##     COMPLETE NETWORK PLOTS PER HOST VARIABLE (FINAL VERSION)              ##
################################################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(Matrix)
  library(igraph); library(graphlayouts); library(ggraph)
  library(ggplot2); library(ggrepel); library(phyloseq); library(stringr)
  library(pheatmap); library(gridExtra); library(RColorBrewer)
})

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##                           USER SETTINGS
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
min_shared_traits <- 2         
layout_algorithm  <- "fr"      # Use FR for more stable layouts
cluster_resolution <- 0.3      
min_community_size <- 2        
min_prevalence <- 0.01         
alpha_threshold <- 0.05        

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##                     ORBL_DIV COLOR SCALE 
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
orbl_colors <- c("#2B5B8A", "#4071A0", "#5789B6", "#6FA3CB", "#A2BCCF", 
                 "#D8D4C9", "#F0AC72", "#EF8530", "#DA6524", "#BE4E21", "#9E3D21")

map_to_orbl_color <- function(pct_positive) {
  color_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(11, round(pct_positive * 10) + 1))
  orbl_colors[color_idx]
}

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##                     PHYLUM COLORS
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
phylum_base_col <- c(
  Firmicutes = "#F38400", Bacteroidetes = "#0067A5", Actinobacteria = "#8DB600",
  Proteobacteria = "#E68FAC", Fusobacteria = "#BE0032", Spirochaetes = "#F3C300",
  Cyanobacteria = "#875692", Acidobacteria = "#F6A600", `Candidate division SR1` = "#2B3D26",
  Planctomycetes = "#332288", Saccharibacteria = "#B3446C", Synergistetes = "#A1CAF1",
  Tenericutes = "#654522", Verrucomicrobia = "#C2B280", unclassified = "#DDDDDD",
  unknown = "#999999", TM7 = "#000000"
)

make_phylum_col <- function(v, alpha = 0.8){
  v[is.na(v) | v == ""] <- "unknown"
  need <- unique(v)
  miss <- setdiff(need, names(phylum_base_col))
  
  extra <- if (length(miss)){
    qual <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(max(3, length(miss)), "Set3")
    setNames(qual[seq_along(miss)], miss)
  } else character(0)
  
  cols <- c(phylum_base_col, extra)[need]
  grDevices::adjustcolor(cols, alpha.f = alpha)
}

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##                     HELPER FUNCTIONS
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
nice <- function(x) gsub("_", " ", x)

tax_tbl <- function(ps){
  df <- as.data.frame(unclass(tax_table(ps)), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df$OTU <- rownames(df)
  df
}

keep_taxa <- function(ps, min_prev = 0.01, trans = "none"){
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix"); if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) m <- t(m)
  ok1 <- if (trans %in% c("clr", "lognorm"))
            apply(m, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
          else
            rowSums(m > 0) / ncol(m) >= min_prev
  ok2 <- !is.na(tax_tbl(ps)$Genus)
  which(ok1 & ok2)
}

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##              NETWORK ANALYSIS FOR ONE HOST VARIABLE (WORKING VERSION)
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
analyze_network_for_variable <- function(sig_res_table, host_var_name, ps, trans, 
                                         min_shared = 2, title_prefix = "") {
  
  cat("    Analyzing:", host_var_name, "...")
  
  # Filter significant results for this specific host variable
  var_associations <- sig_res_table %>%
    filter(
      (dependent_var == host_var_name | independent_var == host_var_name),
      p.value.fdr <= alpha_threshold
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      otu_clean = case_when(
        dependent_var == host_var_name ~ str_remove(independent_var, "_relative$"),
        independent_var == host_var_name ~ str_remove(dependent_var, "_relative$"),
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(otu_clean)) %>%
    select(otu_clean, estimate, p.value.fdr)
  
  cat(sprintf(" found %d associations", nrow(var_associations)))
  
  if (nrow(var_associations) < 3) {
    cat(" - insufficient associations\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create OTU pairs
  otus_in_network <- unique(var_associations$otu_clean)
  cat(sprintf(", %d unique OTUs", length(otus_in_network)))
  
  otu_pairs <- expand.grid(otu1 = otus_in_network, otu2 = otus_in_network, 
                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    filter(otu1 < otu2)
  
  cat(sprintf(", %d potential pairs", nrow(otu_pairs)))
  
  if (nrow(otu_pairs) == 0) {
    cat(" - no pairs possible\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Calculate edge properties - FIXED JOIN
  edge_list <- otu_pairs %>%
    left_join(var_associations, by = c("otu1" = "otu_clean")) %>%
    rename(est1 = estimate, fdr1 = p.value.fdr) %>%
    left_join(var_associations, by = c("otu2" = "otu_clean")) %>%
    rename(est2 = estimate, fdr2 = p.value.fdr) %>%
    filter(!is.na(est1), !is.na(est2)) %>%
    mutate(
      same_direction = sign(est1) == sign(est2),
      both_positive = est1 > 0 & est2 > 0,
      both_negative = est1 < 0 & est2 < 0,
      agreement_score = abs(est1 * est2),
      pct_positive = ifelse(same_direction, 
                           ifelse(both_positive, 1.0, 0.0), 0.5),
      edge_color = map_to_orbl_color(pct_positive),
      layout_weight = ifelse(same_direction, agreement_score * 2, 0.1),
      visual_weight = pmax(0.1, agreement_score)
    )
  
  cat(sprintf(", %d valid edges", nrow(edge_list)))
  
  if (nrow(edge_list) < min_shared) {
    cat(" - insufficient edges\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create node metadata
  taxonomy_df <- tax_tbl(ps)
  
  # Get parsed genus if available
  get_first_existing_column <- function(df, candidates) {
    existing <- candidates[candidates %in% colnames(df)]
    if (length(existing)) existing[1] else NA_character_
  }
  
  genus_col <- get_first_existing_column(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, 
                                        c("parsed_genus", "Genus", "genus"))
  
  nodes_df <- tibble(otu = otus_in_network) %>%
    left_join(select(taxonomy_df, OTU, Phylum_tax = Phylum, Genus_tax = Genus), 
              by = c("otu" = "OTU")) %>%
    # Add parsed genus if available
    {if (!is.na(genus_col)) {
      left_join(., select(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, 
                         otu, parsed_genus = !!sym(genus_col)), 
                by = "otu")
    } else {
      mutate(., parsed_genus = NA_character_)
    }} %>%
    left_join(var_associations, by = c("otu" = "otu_clean")) %>%
    mutate(
      final_phylum = coalesce(Phylum_tax, "unknown"),
      final_genus = coalesce(parsed_genus, Genus_tax, otu),
      association_strength = abs(estimate)
    ) %>%
    select(otu, final_phylum, final_genus, association_strength) %>%
    distinct()
  
  # Apply colors and sizes
  phylum_colors <- make_phylum_col(unique(nodes_df$final_phylum))
  nodes_df <- nodes_df %>%
    mutate(
      node_color = phylum_colors[final_phylum],
      node_size = scales::rescale(association_strength, to = c(3, 10)),
      node_label = final_genus
    ) %>%
    rename(name = otu)
  
  # Build network
  network_graph <- graph_from_data_frame(
    d = select(edge_list, from = otu1, to = otu2, 
               weight = layout_weight, visual_weight, pct_positive),
    vertices = nodes_df, directed = FALSE
  )
  
  # Set attributes
  E(network_graph)$layout_weight <- edge_list$layout_weight
  E(network_graph)$visual_weight <- edge_list$visual_weight
  E(network_graph)$color <- edge_list$edge_color
  E(network_graph)$pct_positive <- edge_list$pct_positive
  
  V(network_graph)$phylum <- nodes_df$final_phylum
  V(network_graph)$genus_label <- nodes_df$final_genus
  V(network_graph)$node_color <- nodes_df$node_color
  V(network_graph)$node_size <- nodes_df$node_size
  
  # Layout
  layout_coords <- layout_with_fr(network_graph, weights = E(network_graph)$layout_weight)
  
  # Create plot
  plot_title <- paste0(title_prefix, " • ", nice(host_var_name))
  plot_subtitle <- sprintf("%d OTUs, %d edges", vcount(network_graph), ecount(network_graph))
  
  network_plot <- ggraph(network_graph, layout = layout_coords) +
    geom_edge_link(aes(width = visual_weight, color = I(color)), alpha = 0.7, lineend = "round") +
    geom_node_point(aes(size = node_size, fill = I(node_color)), shape = 21, stroke = 0.3, color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
    geom_node_text(aes(label = genus_label), size = 2.5, repel = TRUE, max.overlaps = 20, box.padding = 0.3) +
    scale_edge_width_continuous(name = "Association strength", range = c(0.3, 3), guide = "none") +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "Effect size", range = c(3, 12), guide = "none") +
    theme_void(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"), plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)) +
    labs(title = plot_title, subtitle = plot_subtitle, 
         caption = "Edges: blue=opposing, red=agreeing associations to this host variable")
  
  cat(" ✓ network created\n")
  return(network_plot)
}

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##              ANALYZE ONE DATASET (FIXED VERSION)
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
run_network_dataset <- function(ds_name, meta, sig_res_table, ps, trans, out_dir) {
  
  message("== Network analysis: ", nice(ds_name), " (", trans, ")")
  
  # Filter taxa
  keep <- keep_taxa(ps, min_prevalence, trans)
  if (!length(keep)) { 
    message("   no taxa left"); 
    return(list()) 
  }
  
  # Define variables to analyze per dataset
  vars_to_analyze <- switch(ds_name,
    "demoWAS" = c("RIAGENDR"),  # Gender only for now
    "oradWAS" = c("TOOTH_DECAY_OHAROCDT", "GUM_DISEASE_OHAROCGP", "ORAL_HYGIENE_OHAROCOH", "DENTURE_OHAROCDE"),
    "exWAS" = c("DR1TCARB", "DR1TCAFF", "DR1TFIBE", "DR1TALCO"),  # Top variables with associations
    "pheWAS" = c("BMXBMI", "BMXWAIST", "BMXARMC", "BMXWT", "BMXHT", "BPXPLS"),  # Key anthropometric measures
    "outWAS" = c("ASTHMA", "BRONCHITIS", "EMPHYSEMA", "ANGINA", "HEART_FAILURE", "HEART_ATTACK", "STROKE"),
    character(0)  # Default: no variables
  )
  
  if (!length(vars_to_analyze)) { 
    message("   no variables to analyze"); 
    return(list()) 
  }
  
  cat("  Variables to analyze:", paste(vars_to_analyze, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  plot_list <- list()
  
  # FIXED: Process each variable and actually store the results
  for (v in vars_to_analyze) {
    network_plot <- analyze_network_for_variable(
      sig_res_table = sig_res_table,
      host_var_name = v,  
      ps = ps,
      trans = trans,
      min_shared = min_shared_traits,
      title_prefix = paste(nice(ds_name), trans)
    )
    
    # FIXED: Actually store the plot if it was created
    if (!is.null(network_plot)) {
      plot_list[[v]] <- network_plot
      cat("       → Plot stored for", v, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  cat("  → Total plots created:", length(plot_list), "\n")
  return(plot_list)
}

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##                          MASTER EXECUTION LOOP
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
make_dir <- function(x) if (!dir.exists(x)) dir.create(x, recursive = TRUE)

out_root <- "network_plots_final"
make_dir(out_root)

# Use original datasets for correct variable name matching
datasets_corrected <- list(
  demoWAS = sample_data_demoWAS_vars_subset,
  oradWAS = sample_data_oradWAS_vars_subset,
  exWAS   = sample_data_exWAS_vars_subset,
  pheWAS  = sample_data_pheWAS_vars_subset,
  outWAS  = sample_data_outWAS_vars_subset
)

sig_results <- list(
  demoWAS = list(none = demoWAS_none_sig_res, clr = demoWAS_clr_sig_res),
  oradWAS = list(none = oradWAS_none_sig_res, clr = oradWAS_clr_sig_res),
  exWAS = list(none = exWAS_none_sig_res, clr = exWAS_clr_sig_res),
  pheWAS = list(none = pheWAS_none_sig_res, clr = pheWAS_clr_sig_res),
  outWAS = list(none = outWAS_none_sig_res, clr = outWAS_clr_sig_res)
)

phylo_sets <- list(none = ubiome_relative_none, clr = ubiome_relative_clr)

grand_total <- 0

for (tr in names(phylo_sets)) {
  ps <- phylo_sets[[tr]]
  tr_dir <- file.path(out_root, tr)
  make_dir(tr_dir)
  booklet_plots <- list()
  
  for (ds in names(datasets_corrected)) {
    if (ds %in% names(sig_results) && tr %in% names(sig_results[[ds]])) {
      
      plot_list <- run_network_dataset(
        ds_name = ds,
        meta = datasets_corrected[[ds]],
        sig_res_table = sig_results[[ds]][[tr]],
        ps = ps,
        trans = tr,
        out_dir = tr_dir
      )
      
      if (length(plot_list) > 0) {
        cat("  Saving", length(plot_list), "plots for", ds, "\n")
        
        # Save individual plots
        for (var_name in names(plot_list)) {
          filename <- file.path(tr_dir, paste0(ds, "_", var_name, "_", tr, "_network.pdf"))
          cat("    Saving:", filename, "\n")
          
          pdf(filename, 12, 9, family = "Helvetica")
          print(plot_list[[var_name]])
          dev.off()
        }
        
        # Add to booklet
        booklet_plots <- c(booklet_plots, plot_list)
        grand_total <- grand_total + length(plot_list)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Create booklet for this transformation
  if (length(booklet_plots) > 0) {
    booklet_file <- file.path(tr_dir, paste0("ALL_", tr, "_network_booklet.pdf"))
    cat("Creating booklet:", booklet_file, "with", length(booklet_plots), "plots\n")
    
    pdf(booklet_file, 14, 18, family = "Helvetica")
    
    # Print plots 2 per page
    for (i in seq(1, length(booklet_plots), by = 2)) {
      plots_on_page <- booklet_plots[i:min(i+1, length(booklet_plots))]
      if (length(plots_on_page) == 2) {
        grid.arrange(plots_on_page[[1]], plots_on_page[[2]], ncol = 1)
      } else {
        print(plots_on_page[[1]])
      }
    }
    dev.off()
  }
}

## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##                             FINAL SUMMARY
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
message("\n════════ FINAL NETWORK SUMMARY ════════")
message("Total network plots created: ", grand_total)
message("Output directory: ", out_root)
message("")
message("Files created:")
if (dir.exists(out_root)) {
  all_files <- list.files(out_root, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
  for (f in all_files) {
    message("  ", f)
  }
} else {
  message("  No output directory found")
}
message("════════════════════════════════════════")
```

**Key Features of this Network Analysis:**

1. **Per-Variable Networks**: Creates separate network plots for each categorical host variable (like the heatmaps)

2. **OrBl_div Edge Coloring**: 
   - **Blue edges**: OTUs with opposing associations (one positive, one negative)
   - **Red edges**: OTUs with agreeing associations (both positive or both negative)  
   - **Intermediate colors**: Based on percentage agreement

3. **Same Structure as Heatmaps**:
   - Loops through datasets (demoWAS, oradWAS, exWAS, pheWAS, outWAS)
   - Loops through transformations (none, clr, lognorm)
   - Filters variables with sufficient levels and samples
   - Creates individual PDFs + combined booklets

4. **Meaningful Edge Relationships**: Instead of arbitrary "shared traits", edges represent **association agreement** - whether two OTUs respond similarly or oppositely to the same host variable

5. **Phylum-Based Node Colors**: Uses the same phylum color palette as the heatmaps

6. **Community Detection**: Groups OTUs with similar association patterns

This creates interpretable networks where **each plot answers**: *"Which OTUs respond similarly vs. oppositely to this specific host variable?"*



```{R}
variable_description %>% DT::datatable(options = list(
  scrollX=TRUE, scrollY=TRUE,
  initComplete = htmlwidgets::JS(
    "function(settings, json) {",
    paste0("$(this.api().table().container()).css({'font-size': '8pt'});"),"}")))
```

```{R}
################################################################################
## Differential-abundance heat-maps + CSV + booklet (2024-06 – taxonomy fix)  ##
################################################################################
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(phyloseq)
  library(pheatmap)
  library(gridExtra)
  library(RColorBrewer)
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
})

###############################################################################
## 1.  colours & palette                                                      ##
###############################################################################
phylum_base_col <- c(
  Firmicutes               = "#F38400",
  Bacteroidetes            = "#0067A5",
  Actinobacteria           = "#8DB600",
  Proteobacteria           = "#E68FAC",
  Fusobacteria             = "#BE0032",
  Spirochaetes             = "#F3C300",
  Cyanobacteria            = "#875692",
  Acidobacteria            = "#F6A600",
  `Candidate division SR1` = "#2B3D26",
  Planctomycetes           = "#332288",
  Saccharibacteria         = "#B3446C",
  Synergistetes            = "#A1CAF1",
  Tenericutes              = "#654522",
  Verrucomicrobia          = "#C2B280",
  unclassified             = "#DDDDDD",
  unknown                  = "#999999",
  TM7                      = "#000000"
)

colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#053061", "#F7F7F7", "#67001F"))(100)

breaks_fixed <- list(
  clr     = seq(-2.5,  2.5, length.out = 101),
  none    = seq(-1.5,  1.5, length.out = 101),
  lognorm = seq(-1,  1, length.out = 101)
)

###############################################################################
## 2.  helpers                                                                ##
###############################################################################
nice    <- function(x) gsub("_", " ", x)

tax_tbl <- function(ps){                         # --- FIXED -------------
  df <- as.data.frame(unclass(tax_table(ps)), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df$OTU <- rownames(df)
  df
}

keep_taxa <- function(ps, min_prev = 0.01, trans = "none"){
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix"); if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) m <- t(m)
  ok1 <- if (trans %in% c("clr", "lognorm"))
            apply(m, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
          else
            rowSums(m > 0) / ncol(m) >= min_prev
  ok2 <- !is.na(tax_tbl(ps)$Genus)
  which(ok1 & ok2)
}

p_one <- function(x, g){
  tryCatch({
    ok <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(g)
    if (sum(ok) < 4) return(1)
    x <- x[ok]; g <- droplevels(g[ok])
    if (nlevels(g) < 2 || var(x) == 0) return(1)
    if (nlevels(g) == 2) suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(x ~ g)$p.value)
    else                  kruskal.test(x, g)$p.value
  }, error = function(e) 1)
}

###############################################################################
## helper that returns 65-%-opaque phylum colours                             ##
###############################################################################
make_phylum_col <- function(v, alpha = 0.65){
  v[is.na(v) | v == ""] <- "unknown"

  need <- unique(v)
  miss <- setdiff(need, names(phylum_base_col))

  extra <- if (length(miss)){
    qual <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(max(3, length(miss)), "Set3")
    setNames(qual[seq_along(miss)], miss)
  } else character(0)

  cols <- c(phylum_base_col, extra)[need]
  ## add the alpha channel
  cols <- grDevices::adjustcolor(cols, alpha.f = alpha)

  ## keep names so pheatmap can match them
  setNames(cols, need)
}

###############################################################################
##  wrapper around pheatmap  (1/4 height, safe cutree)                        ##
###############################################################################
# wrapper around pheatmap  (1/8 height, safe cutree)
###############################################################################
##  pheatmap wrapper – fixed legend ticks                                     ##
###############################################################################
draw_hm <- function(mat, title, key, anno_row, anno_cols){

  # 1/8 of heat-map height, ≥ 5 px
  h <- max(5, ceiling(nrow(mat) * 5 / 16))

  brks <- breaks_fixed[[key]]
  lgd  <- c(min(brks), 0, max(brks))               # show min, 0, max

  args <- list(
    mat,
    cluster_rows  = nrow(mat) > 1,
    clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
    cluster_cols = FALSE,
    treeheight_row = h,
    color  = colors,
    breaks = brks,
    legend_breaks  = lgd,
    legend_labels  = format(lgd, trim = TRUE),
    annotation_row    = anno_row,
    annotation_colors = anno_cols,
    cellwidth  = 5.5,
    cellheight = 5.5,
    fontsize   = 6,
    fontsize_row = 6,
    fontsize_col = 6,
    main = title,
    silent = TRUE
  )
  if (nrow(mat) > 3) args$cutree_rows <- 4

  do.call(pheatmap, args)
}
###############################################################################
## 3. analyse one factor                                                      ##
###############################################################################
analyze_factor <- function(otu, g, ps, trans,
                           top_n = 30, alpha = 0.05){

  p_vec <- apply(otu, 1, p_one, g = g)
  q_vec <- p.adjust(p_vec, "BH")

  levels_g <- levels(g)
  means_all <- sapply(levels_g, \(lv)
      rowMeans(otu[, g == lv, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE))
  if (is.null(dim(means_all)))
      means_all <- matrix(means_all, nrow = 1,
                          dimnames = list(rownames(otu), levels_g))

  tx <- tax_tbl(ps)[match(rownames(otu), tax_tbl(ps)$OTU), ]
  genus  <- ifelse(is.na(tx$Genus)|tx$Genus%in%c("", "unclassified"),
                   paste0("Uncl ", tx$OTU), nice(tx$Genus))
  phylum <- ifelse(is.na(tx$Phylum)|tx$Phylum=="", "unknown", tx$Phylum)

  res_tab <- data.frame(
    OTU      = tx$OTU,
    Genus    = genus,
    Phylum   = phylum,
    p_value  = p_vec,
    p_adj_BH = q_vec,
    means_all,
    in_top_panel = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  sig_idx <- which(q_vec <= alpha); if (!length(sig_idx))
      return(list(plots=list(), table=res_tab))

  means_sig <- means_all[sig_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  rng <- apply(means_sig, 1, \(z) diff(range(z, na.rm = TRUE)))
  keep <- head(order(rng, decreasing = TRUE),
               min(top_n, length(rng)))
  res_tab$in_top_panel[sig_idx[keep]] <- TRUE
  means_top <- means_sig[keep, , drop = FALSE]

  row_lab <- make.unique(genus[sig_idx][keep])
  rownames(means_top) <- row_lab
  phylum_top <- phylum[sig_idx][keep]
  anno_r <- data.frame(Phylum = phylum_top, row.names = row_lab)
  anno_c <- list(Phylum = make_phylum_col(phylum_top))

  if (trans == "clr"){
      panel <- sweep(means_top, 1, means_top[,1], "-") / log(2)
      plt   <- draw_hm(panel, paste(nice(attr(g,"varname")), "log₂ fold change"),
                       "clr", anno_r, anno_c)
      return(list(plots = list(log2FC = plt), table = res_tab))
  }

  if (trans == "lognorm"){
      panel <- sweep(means_top, 1, means_top[,1], "-")
      plt   <- draw_hm(panel, paste(nice(attr(g,"varname")), "log₁₀ diff"),
                       "lognorm", anno_r, anno_c)
      return(list(plots = list(log10Diff = plt), table = res_tab))
  }

  panel <- t(scale(t(means_top)))
  plt   <- draw_hm(panel, paste(nice(attr(g,"varname")), "z-score"),
                   "none", anno_r, anno_c)
  list(plots = list(Abundance = plt), table = res_tab)
}

###############################################################################
## 4. run one dataset                                                         ##
###############################################################################
run_dataset <- function(ds_name, meta, ps, trans, out_dir,
                        min_prev = 0.01, top_n = 30, alpha = 0.05){

  message("== ", nice(ds_name), " (", trans, ")")
  keep <- keep_taxa(ps, min_prev, trans)
  if (!length(keep)){ message("   no taxa left"); return(list()) }
  ps   <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(ps)[keep], ps)

  otu <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix"); if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) otu <- t(otu)
  common <- intersect(colnames(otu), meta$SEQN)
  if (length(common) < 30){ message("   too few samples"); return(list()) }
  otu  <- otu[, common]
  meta <- meta |> filter(SEQN %in% common) |> slice(match(common, SEQN))

  fac_vars <- setdiff(names(Filter(is.factor, meta)), "SEQN")
  if (!length(fac_vars)){ message("   no factor vars"); return(list()) }

  pl_all <- list()
  for (v in fac_vars){
    g <- droplevels(meta[[v]])
    if (nlevels(g) < 2 || sum(!is.na(g)) < 30){
      message("   ", nice(v), " skipped"); next
    }
    attr(g,"varname") <- v
    ana <- analyze_factor(otu, g, ps, trans, top_n, alpha)

    write.csv(ana$table,
              file.path(out_dir,
                        paste0(ds_name, "_", v, "_", trans, "_table.csv")),
              row.names = FALSE)

    if (length(ana$plots)){
      nm <- names(ana$plots)[1]
      pl_all[[paste0(v, "_", nm)]] <- ana$plots[[1]]
      message("   ", nice(v), " ✔")
    } else message("   ", nice(v), " – none")
  }
  pl_all
}

###############################################################################
## 5. master loop                                                             ##
###############################################################################
make_dir <- function(x) if (!dir.exists(x)) dir.create(x, TRUE)

out_root <- file.path(viz_out_path, "categories_heatmap_fixed"); make_dir(out_root)

datasets <- list(
  demoWAS = demoWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap,
  oradWAS = oradWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap,
  exWAS   = exWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap,
  pheWAS  = pheWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmap,
  outWAS  = outdWAS_vars_subset_factorized_for_heatmapheatmap
)

phylo_sets <- list(
  none    = ubiome_relative_none,
  clr     = ubiome_relative_clr,
  lognorm = ubiome_relative_lognorm
)

grand_total <- 0

for (tr in names(phylo_sets)){
  ps     <- phylo_sets[[tr]]
  tr_dir <- file.path(out_root, tr); make_dir(tr_dir)
  booklet <- list()

  for (ds in names(datasets)){
    pl <- run_dataset(ds, datasets[[ds]], ps, tr, tr_dir)
    if (length(pl)){
      for (nm in names(pl)){
        pdf(file.path(tr_dir, paste0(ds, "_", nm, "_", tr, ".pdf")),
            10, 7, family = "Helvetica")
        print(pl[[nm]]); dev.off()
      }
      booklet <- c(booklet, pl)
      grand_total <- grand_total + length(pl)
    }
  }
  if (length(booklet)){
    # flatten to a pure list of gtables
    flat <- Filter(function(z) inherits(z, "gtable"),
                   unlist(booklet, recursive = TRUE))

    pdf(file.path(tr_dir, paste0("ALL_", tr, "_booklet.pdf")),
        12, 18, family = "Helvetica")
    print(marrangeGrob(flat, ncol = 2, nrow = 3))
    dev.off()
  }
}

message("\n======== SUMMARY =========")
message("Total individual PDFs: ", grand_total)
message("Outputs (PDFs + CSVs) in: ", out_root)
```
